items.title: S&P 500 up 4%+ in both April AND May — this pattern has led to gains 5 of 6 times, avg +14.7% over next 6 months

items.source: David Rosenberg / Early Morning With Dave
items.title: Most concentrated S&P 500 ever: CAPE at 41 (Sept 2000 high), ERP at -25bps, IG spreads tightest since 1998 Russian collapse
PIMCO: The Quiet Erosion Beneath US Growth — real disposable income down 1.1% YoY, core labor income per worker -0.9% (recession pace)
Fed hike probability climbed to 81% by year-end (from 57% Friday) — June FOMC to drop easing bias; ADP +122K best since January 2025
Initial jobless claims +13k to 225K (10K above expected); Fed Beige Book: low-hire, low-fire, workers reluctant to change jobs
Fed Beige Book: US at 2% growth at best; consumer bifurcation deepens; agricultural loan delinquencies rising; energy driving margin compression
Yardeni flags June Swoon: S&P rallied 19.1% from March 30 low; forward earnings +26.6% YoY; oil could spike to $150 without quick Iran resolution

items.source: Challenger, Gray & Christmas / The Bond Beat
items.title: AI drives May layoffs to 97,006 — highest May total since 2020; AI is cited reason #1 for the THIRD consecutive month; Tech cuts highest since 2023
Most of the economy won't run on the best AI model — inference costs fell 280x in 2 years; hyperscalers win in BOTH capex scenarios; market mispriced
The Year the Magnificent Seven Stopped Being Magnificent — ATHs everywhere, Mag7 nowhere; capex explodes, cash flow disappoints, valuations at 10-year lows
The Trade Everyone Missed — SOX +45%, IGV +43%: software quietly matched semiconductor returns while nobody watched
Beginner's Guide to Reopening the Strait: 13-15 mbpd shut-in, 200 tankers with 160M barrels are a stockpile NOT a supply offset, 6 months to clear mines
Canada's Newfoundland holds 27.6 TCF of recoverable natural gas ($400B) — Europe seeking energy diversification; Carney faces massive political/regulatory hurdle
Three Body Problem as lens on CRINK worldview: Only hard truth is survival. Opposing civilizations are blood-drenched pyramids in a dark forest.

items.source: The Dark Side of the Boom
items.title: Dollar winning on points not by knockout: EUR/USD stuck at 1.1600, USD/JPY near 160 — two forces restraining what should be a runaway winner

items.source: Unchained Crypto / Lucky Trader / Mando Minutes
items.title: BTC below $62K — erases entire Iran rally; -50%+ from $126K ATH; 11 consecutive days of ETF outflows ($3.83B since May); approaching $60K cycle low
Arthur Hayes sold his entire HYPE and NEAR positions — cites higher energy prices + 3 AI IPOs + Trump AI pivot as reasons for market top now to September
BitMine (Tom Lee) files $300M preferred stock at 9.5% to buy ETH — holds 5.4M ETH with $300M/yr staking income; structural contrast to Saylor's STRC
HYPE briefly flipped SOL in dollar price ($75 vs $72) — Hyperliquid at 6.63% of global perp futures; Grayscale launches HYPG ETF at 0.15% fee
Bessent wants CLARITY Act passed this summer; Mastercard launches 24/7 stablecoin settlement (USDC, RLUSD, PYUSD); Stripe + Visa + MC backing joint stablecoin platform
DOJ/CFTC first-ever insider trading case: Google engineer made $1.2M on Polymarket using nonpublic search ranking data
China hits the brakes on gold: PBoC removed RMB 1.8 trillion ($260B) of liquidity in 12 weeks; gold in CNY testing critical RMB 30,000/oz support

items.source: The Dark Side of the Boom / UBS
items.title: UBS: Sovereign gold buying began in 2008, not 2022 — Russia sanctions accelerated an existing trend; a handful of countries drive ALL net accumulation
TME: Dare some protection — QQQ July 730/690 put spread offers 4.5x; The market has forgotten how to go down
JPM: China tech = most underowned trade in global tech; APAC version of IGV; breakout has started but crowd still isn't there
Broadcom party pooper: AI rev +143% but stock -13% after-hours; Google equity raise to $84.75B; Meta eyes $200/month Hatch AI agent
MoonPay MoonAgents: Claude Code + OpenAI Codex can now trade crypto on users behalf — 54 crypto tools, 17 skills, 10 chains, keys stored locally
Morning Reality Check 6/4: Nasdaq -1%, S&P -0.4%; oil -3% on Lebanon ceasefire; Gold rallying as Bitcoin falls below $64K; 10yr at 4.45%
North Korea unveils new nuclear fuel facility; US oil reserves at lowest since 2004; House votes to curb Trump Iran war powers; no tangible peace progress

items.source: Bespoke Invest / Bond Beat / Mando Minutes
items.title: S&P near ATH at 7500; services PMI at annualized pace just above 1% Q2; consumer-facing service orders falling at steepest pace since 2020
